title: Can't re-install podman after trying GPU acceleration - Error: exec: "krunkit": executable file not found in $PATH
body:
### Issue Description

I was trying to get GPU usable from inside containers, following [the docs](https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/gpu).

I got it working:

```bash
Marks-MacBook-Air:content-source mjgs$ podman run --rm -it --device /dev/dri --name gpu-info localhost/fedora-sudo vulkaninfo | grep "GPU"
		GPU id = 0 (Virtio-GPU Venus (Apple M4))
		GPU id = 1 (llvmpipe (LLVM 18.1.6, 128 bits))
GPU0:
	deviceType        = PHYSICAL_DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU
	deviceName        = Virtio-GPU Venus (Apple M4)
GPU1:
```

But then realized somehow the new configuration had messed up all my existing projects. When I reopen old known good projects inside a VSCode devcontainer, git shows all files as having been modified, not the content just meta data. Discovered that podman was running in rootful mode, which I don't remember changing to. Decided to re-install everything, so removed all krunkit stuff I had brew installed. Now podman doesn't install.

How can I re-install podman and get all my projects opening correctly again in VSCode devcontainers?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Try to configure GPU acceleration https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/gpu
2. Get it working and discover git is broken for all your projects inside devcontainer
3. Uninstall everything you installed along the way
4. Stop podman machine
5. Delete podman machine
6. Uninstall podman using brew
7. Re-install podman using brew

### Describe the results you received

Podman installs, and podman machine is created, but can't start podman machine:

```bash
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ brew list | grep podman
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ brew list --cask | grep podman
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ brew install podman
==> Fetching downloads for: podman
==> Downloading https://ghcr.io/v2/homebrew/core/podman/manifests/5.6.1
Already downloaded: /Users/mjgs/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/50bbdc19ff1ca5c18a5ec7a178a34eb824ff97bd10b4d6de897a88bd28ea52dd--podman-5.6.1.bottle_manifest.json
==> Fetching podman
==> Downloading https://ghcr.io/v2/homebrew/core/podman/blobs/sha256:6c5159a90a364a86870db91f498f72775b94f7acfde18b978bda9d59589d7cbb
Already downloaded: /Users/mjgs/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/4886fb48fd18eba989c58effe61f23e66ec58239c1914278311056987baa34ef--podman--5.6.1.arm64_sequoia.bottle.tar.gz
==> Pouring podman--5.6.1.arm64_sequoia.bottle.tar.gz
==> Caveats
In order to run containers locally, podman depends on a Linux kernel.
One can be started manually using `podman machine` from this package.
To start a podman VM automatically at login, also install the cask
"podman-desktop".
==> Summary
🍺  /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/5.6.1: 217 files, 75.7MB
==> Running `brew cleanup podman`...
Disable this behaviour by setting `HOMEBREW_NO_INSTALL_CLEANUP=1`.
Hide these hints with `HOMEBREW_NO_ENV_HINTS=1` (see `man brew`).
==> No outdated dependents to upgrade!
==> Caveats
Bash completion has been installed to:
  /opt/homebrew/etc/bash_completion.d
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ podman machine init

Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.6 to create VM
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start

Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
ERRO[0000] process 5238 has not ended
Error: exec: "krunkit": executable file not found in $PATH
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ podman --version
podman version 5.6.1
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected to be able to start the new podman machine.

### podman info output

```yaml
Marks-MacBook-Air:~ mjgs$ podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
buildOrigin: brew
provider: libkrun
version: 5.6.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:63458: connect: connection refused
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
If you have install issues with brew or missing deps you must report them to brew not upstream, we have no control over what brew ships.

WE recommend using our own installer which contains all the required deps https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.6.1
Thanks - I opened a [discussion on the homebrew repo](https://github.com/orgs/Homebrew/discussions/6401).
